title: IBKR Portfolio Report v2 — Long/Short Metals Book

tradeTape.note: The book was rebuilt from the original 6-name long-only lithium basket into a 25-name long/short metals book - long lithium & specialty-materials producers vs. short precious/PGM, steel, aluminum and battery/EV ETFs. Rows show the largest current holdings (current share count at average cost - not intraday fills; live trade history needs an approved IBKR pull).

etfLookThrough.concentrationNote: Long lithium producers (+9.4% of NAV) while short the BATT/LIT/KARS ETFs (-10.1%) that themselves hold those same miners - a partial internal self-hedge. HHI 578 (~17.3 effective names): far more diversified than the retired 6-name book (HHI 1,924 / ~5.2). Largest single position is the off-theme BEPC renewables short at 9.7% of NAV - roughly 2x the next name.

metrics.note: from the account's daily TWR, 21 daily obs since inception
trailing 90d daily returns, 25 of 25 holdings (0 OTC ex.)

scenarios.name: Steel complex -15%
scenarios.note: net short NUE/STLD/CLF/RS/CMC -> gain · first-order est

scenarios.name: Lithium/battery +20%
scenarios.note: longs ~= short ETFs after the trim - near flat · est

scenarios.name: Precious/PGM +20% squeeze
scenarios.note: short silver/PGM pain - now the largest bucket · est

drivers.read: Headwind - steel shorts NUE/STLD/CLF/RS/CMC (~19% of gross)
Mixed — long ALB/SQM/LAC/SGML vs short BATT/LIT/KARS

driversNote: Net-short-friendly backdrop: precious metals (silver -21% 3M, gold -14%, PGM in surplus) and aluminum are correcting - a tailwind to the book's short precious/PGM/aluminum leg, now the largest bucket (~20% of gross) - while steel stays firm (HRC >$1,100 on 50% S232 tariffs), a headwind to the steel shorts (~19% of gross). Lithium is two-sided. Macro: the Fed is on a dovish hold at 3.63%, CPI has eased to 3.46%, the curve is normal (10Y-2Y +0.41) and the VIX is calm at 16.7.

smartMoney.detail: Buy consensus, mean PT ~$207 (+72% upside), SmartScore 9 - supports the long lithium leg (though price momentum is weak).
Buy consensus, PT ~$270 (+14%), +27% vs 200DMA, composite 80.6 - the steel short is fighting a strong tape; the highest-rated steel name is the wrong one to be short.

options.note: The book holds no options - it is cash equities/ETFs, net short -43% of NAV. There is no embedded vega or convexity; the risk is linear and directional, captured by the scenarios above.

performance.note: Since inception (2026-06-22) the account's time-weighted return is -11.3%. Over the same window SPY was roughly flat (-0.1%) and the metals ETF XME fell -14.6% - the book tracked the metals complex down through the transition but fell less than XME. The sample is ~4 weeks and spans the rebuild, so treat this as indicative, not a track record.

bottomLine: The book was rebuilt from a concentrated long-lithium bet into a diversified 25-name long/short metals book: net short -43% of NAV, gross ~62%, ~17.3 effective names (HHI 578 vs the old 1,924). Realised vol is ~18.5% (about half the old book's 38%) and metals beta is low (XME 0.19) as the long/short structure neutralises directional metals exposure; SPY beta is ~0.66. Since inception (Jun 22) the account is -11.3% TWR vs XME -14.6%. The book PROFITS from a metals selloff (steel -15% ~ +$246, broad base metals -10% ~ +$469) and is hurt by a precious/PGM squeeze (+20% ~ -$350), now the largest bucket (~20% of gross) after the deleverage. The single largest position is the off-theme BEPC renewables short (9.7% of NAV, top risk contributor) - flagged for review. Watch steel Q2 earnings (CLF Jul 23, NUE/STLD late Jul), the Jul 28-29 FOMC and the lithium longs' prints (ALB early Aug, SQM Aug 19).
sources: IBKR (live account, refreshed 2026-07-20) · FMP · TipRanks · our micro.json commodity-bias & analyst layer · macro feeds. Risk metrics computed from the account's daily TWR series since inception (2026-06-22, 21 daily obs) - a short sample spanning the book's rebuild, so vol/beta are indicative. Scenario P&L are first-order estimates (miner beta ~1.3). Informational only - not investment advice.
